1.texto_original: OR Israele uscì in battaglia incontro a’ Filistei, e si accampò presso ad Eben-ezer; e i Filistei si accamparono in Afec.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of 1 Samuele 4:1

"OR Israele uscì in battaglia incontro a’ Filistei, e si accampò presso ad Eben-ezer; e i Filistei si accamparono in Afec."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 1 Samuele 4:1

Philological Analysis

"OR Israele uscì in battaglia incontro a’ Filistei, e si accampò presso ad Eben-ezer; e i Filistei si accamparono in Afec."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"OR Israele uscì in battaglia incontro a’ Filistei, e si accampò presso ad Eben-ezer; e i Filistei si accamparono in Afec."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

2.texto_original: E i Filistei ordinarono la battaglia incontro ad Israele. E, datasi la battaglia, Israele fu sconfitto davanti a’ Filistei; ed essi ne percossero intorno a quattromila uomini in battaglia ordinata, nella campagna.

3.texto_original: Ed essendo il popolo venuto nel campo, gli Anziani d’Israele dissero: Perchè ci ha il Signore oggi sconfitti davanti a’ Filistei? facciamoci portar da Silo l’Arca del Patto del Signore, e venga egli nel mezzo di noi, e salvici, dalla mano de’ nostri nemici.

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"E, come egli giunse, ecco, Eli sedeva in sul seggio, allato alla strada, e stava a riguardare; perciocchè il cuor gli tremava per cagion dell’Arca di Dio. Quell’uomo adunque entrò nella città, portando le novelle, e tutta la città fece un gran grido."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

14.texto_original: Ed Eli, udendo il romor delle grida disse: Che vuol dire il romore di questo tumulto? E quell’uomo andò prestamente ad Eli, e gli raccontò la cosa.
14.normal:
Devotional Study of 1 Samuele 4:14

"Ed Eli, udendo il romor delle grida disse: Che vuol dire il romore di questo tumulto? E quell’uomo andò prestamente ad Eli, e gli raccontò la cosa."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
14.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 1 Samuele 4:14

Philological Analysis

"Ed Eli, udendo il romor delle grida disse: Che vuol dire il romore di questo tumulto? E quell’uomo andò prestamente ad Eli, e gli raccontò la cosa."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

19.texto_original: E la sua nuora, moglie di Finees, essendo gravida, presta a partorire, udì il grido che l’Arca di Dio era presa, e che il suo suocero, e il suo marito erano morti; onde ella si accosciò, e partorì; perciocchè i dolori le sopraggiunsero ad un tratto.
